source_jobs.title: Backend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BEVEL

Bevel is the #1 AI Health Companion with over 1 million downloads globally. We help people make smarter decisions about their health every day by bringing together data across sleep, recovery, activity, nutrition, and more, and turning it into real-time, personalized guidance. Whether you're training for something big or just trying to feel better, Bevel helps you move with clarity and confidence.



We’re a small team of builders and creatives based in NYC. We are extremely passionate about our craft and view Bevel as part of our life’s work. We care deeply about maintaining harmony in all aspects of life and know when to work hard and when to take breaks.



Bevel launched in early 2024, and we’ve been lucky to see incredible growth and support from our users. We’re looking for incredible Backend Engineers to help us build an ambitious roadmap https://feedback.bevel.health. You’ll get an opportunity to shape the future of Bevel and have full ownership and creative freedom to create your life’s work.




RESPONSIBILITIES 

 - Ship features across the entire backend stack, from data infra to product endpoints

 - Build extensible and robust business logic for processing health data

 - Prototype, productionize, and run experiments to improve accuracy, speed, and cost of AI/ML models.

 - Establish best practices for scalability, security, testing, and ML ops (model versioning, monitoring, alerting)

 - Continuously iterate on the engineering process and culture




REQUIREMENTS

 - At least 2 years of experience

 - Experience designing, building, and running a large-scale backend system

 - Expertise coding in Typescript or Python

 - Strong intuition for algorithms and working with data

 - Brilliant at math and statistics

 - Obsessive with your craft

 - Great at communicating ideas

 - A strong bias for action

 - Know when to ask for help and how to help others

 - Excited about improving the health & fitness of others
   
   


BENEFITS

 - Exciting work environment

 - Competitive salary & equity

 - Health, dental, and vision insurance

 - Commuter benefits

 - Paid lunch and dinner when in the office

 - Work-life balance

 - Pet-friendly office in Chelsea

 - Unlimited PTO

 - 401k plan

 - Visa sponsorship if applicable

⠀

Bevel celebrates and embraces diversity and is committed to building a team that represents a variety of experiences, backgrounds, and skills. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, marital status, age, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, non-disqualifying physical or mental disability, national origin, veteran status, or other applicable legally protected characteristics.

source_jobs.title: iOS Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BEVEL

Bevel is the #1 AI Health Companion with over 1 million downloads globally. We help people make smarter decisions about their health every day by bringing together data across sleep, recovery, activity, nutrition, and more, and turning it into real-time, personalized guidance. Whether you're training for something big or just trying to feel better, Bevel helps you move with clarity and confidence.



We are a small, fast-moving team based in NYC, passionate about building beautiful and functional products. We are constantly looking for creative ways to push the capabilities of iOS/watchOS to create an app that is more intelligent, fun, and integrated into our users’ lives. 



Bevel launched in early 2024, and we’ve been lucky to see incredible growth and support from our users. We’re looking to bring on additional iOS engineers to help us build an ambitious roadmap https://feedback.bevel.health/. You’ll get an opportunity to shape the future of Bevel and have full ownership and creative freedom to create your life’s work.




RESPONSIBILITIES 

 - Build new features and improvements across the Bevel iOS and watchOS apps

 - Implement clean, efficient, and well-tested Swift code

 - Collaborate with design and product to bring delightful user experiences to life

 - Work with HealthKit, WatchKit, and SwiftUI to integrate health and activity data

 - Debug performance issues and optimize for speed, reliability, and battery efficiency

 - Participate in code reviews and contribute to technical discussions

 - Help improve our app architecture, tooling, and development processes as we scale




REQUIREMENTS

 - 2–4 years of experience building native iOS apps

 - Proficiency in Swift and SwiftUI

 - Familiarity with Apple frameworks like HealthKit, WatchKit, Combine, and Core Data

 - Understanding of app architecture patterns (MVC, MVVM, etc.)

 - A good sense of design and appreciation for detail

 - Comfortable working independently in a fast-paced environment

 - Strong communication skills and collaborative mindset

 - Excited about health, fitness, and helping people improve their lives
   
   


BENEFITS

 - Exciting work environment

 - Competitive salary & equity

 - Health, dental, and vision insurance

 - Commuter benefits

 - Paid lunch and dinner

 - Work-life harmony

 - Pet-friendly office in Chelsea

 - Unlimited PTO

 - 401(k) plan

 - Visa sponsorship if applicable

⠀

Bevel celebrates and embraces diversity and is committed to building a team that represents a variety of experiences, backgrounds, and skills. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, marital status, age, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, non-disqualifying physical or mental disability, national origin, veteran status, or other applicable legally protected characteristics.

source_jobs.title: Senior iOS Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BEVEL

Bevel is the #1 AI Health Companion with over 1 million downloads globally. We help people make smarter decisions about their health every day by bringing together data across sleep, recovery, activity, nutrition, and more, and turning it into real-time, personalized guidance. Whether you're training for something big or just trying to feel better, Bevel helps you move with clarity and confidence.



We are a small, fast-moving team based in NYC, passionate about building beautiful and functional products. We are constantly looking for creative ways to push the capabilities of iOS/watchOS to create an app that is more intelligent, fun, and integrated into our users’ lives. 



Bevel launched in early 2024, and we’ve been lucky to see incredible growth and support from our users. We’re looking to bring on a Senior iOS Engineer to help us build an ambitious roadmap https://feedback.bevel.health/. You’ll get an opportunity to shape the future of Bevel and have full ownership and creative freedom to create your life’s work.




RESPONSIBILITIES 

 - Lead major features and architecture decisions across iOS and watchOS

 - Collaborate with design, product, and marketing to deliver world-class experiences

 - Build high-quality, maintainable code with strong attention to performance and detail

 - Push the limits of Apple’s frameworks — HealthKit, WatchKit, SwiftUI, etc.

 - Mentor other engineers, share best practices, and raise the engineering bar

 - Contribute to the evolution of Bevel’s app architecture and internal tools

 - Help define and refine our development process as we scale




REQUIREMENTS

 - 4+ years building native iOS apps with Swift and SwiftUI

 - Deep understanding of Apple’s frameworks, app lifecycle, and performance optimization

 - Experience shipping high-quality, design-centric consumer apps

 - Strong fundamentals in algorithms, data structures, and concurrency

 - A strong product mindset

 - Excellent communication and collaboration skills

 - A bias for action, ownership, and shipping

 - Excited about helping people live healthier, better lives
   
   


BENEFITS

 - Exciting work environment

 - Competitive salary & equity

 - Health, dental, and vision insurance

 - Commuter benefits

 - Paid lunch and dinner when in the office

 - Work-life balance

 - Pet-friendly office in Chelsea

 - Unlimited PTO

 - 401k plan

 - Visa sponsorship if applicable

⠀

Bevel celebrates and embraces diversity and is committed to building a team that represents a variety of experiences, backgrounds, and skills. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, marital status, age, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, non-disqualifying physical or mental disability, national origin, veteran status, or other applicable legally protected characteristics.
